title: Typography
description: Typography in ACoreX is very straightforward and follows web standards. To add a font family, simply use the standard method.Additionally, we’ve provided a set of ready-to-use classes that you can apply to headings and subheadings.

sections.title: Font families
sections.description: The $theme-colors variable is an array of colors, where each color can have a range — specifying a start color and an end color.It also supports different color formats such as oklch, rgb, and hex.The color names are reserved and include the following:

sections.title: Headings Classes
sections.description: By using the ready-made heading classes, you can easily apply different headings based on the ACoreX design system — without needing to add any extra styles.

data.content:
Heading 1

Heading 2

Heading 3

Heading 4

Heading 5
Heading 6

sections.title: Subtitle class
sections.description: Using the subtitle class, you can add subtitles to headings.Each type of heading has only one subtitle, and its style does not vary depending on the heading type.The subtitle features a standard font size and a color with a lower intensity level.

sections.title: Heading/Subtitle Component
sections.description: In addition to Tailwind classes, ACoreX also includes a ax-heading component that offers greater flexibility.to use this component, you need to import the AXDecoratorHeadingComponent , AXDecoratorGenericComponent in your module.
